member.name: Chris McDonald
member.synopsis: Chris McDonald is the Labour MP for Stockton North , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently holds the Government posts of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Department for Energy Security and Net Zero), and Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Department for Business and Trade).

writtenQuestions.heading: Terrorism: Northern Ireland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, with reference to his oral contribution of 26 February 2025, Official Report, column 765 and the judgment in R v Adams (Appellant) (Northern Ireland) [2020] UKSC 19, what steps he is taking to address th
writtenQuestions.answerText: The main issue here is the application of the Carltona principle in the context of ICOs. The previous Government’s attempt to address this following the 2020 Supreme Court judgment in Adams has been found by the Northern Ireland courts to be unlawful and ...

writtenQuestions.heading: India: Pakistan
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps he is taking to encourage India to meet its obligations under the Indus Waters Treaty.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is aware that the Government of India has placed the Indus Water Treaty into abeyance due to ongoing tensions between India and Pakistan. It is critical for all countries and international partners to work together to ensure the long-term sustainab...

writtenQuestions.heading: Infrastructure: Seas and Oceans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to protect critical undersea infrastructure.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Cables and pipelines are as vital for our economy as factories and power stations.This Government is increasing defence spending to keep Britain safe at home and secure abroad. And that includes investing in RFA Proteus to protect our undersea cables.

writtenQuestions.heading: Offensive Weapons: Prosecutions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Solicitor General, what steps she is taking to help ensure the effective prosecution of cases involving the use of offensive weapons.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government’s priority is to keep our streets safe, that is why we have committed to halve knife crime in a decade as part of our Safer Streets Mission. As well as committing to more police officers and police community support officers on our streets...

writtenQuestions.heading: Disabled Facilities Grants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he plans to increase the maximum application amount for the Disabled Facilities Grant.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are giving older and disabled people more independence in their own homes through an immediate in-year uplift to the Disabled Facilities Grant (DFG) of £86 million in 2024/25. This increase will provide approximately 7,800 additional home adaptations. ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Minerals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether he has made a comparative assessment of the (a) effectiveness and (b) extent of the UK's list of critical minerals and the list used by (a) the European Union and (ii) other jurisdictions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Critical Minerals Intelligence Centre (CMIC), sponsored by the Department for Business and Trade, will publish an updated assessment of the criticality of over 80 candidate materials, later this year. This assessment is focused on the UK's circumstanc...

writtenQuestions.heading: Minerals

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Critical Minerals Intelligence Centre (CMIC), sponsored by the Department for Business and Trade, is currently undertaking an updated criticality assessment, with the results to be released later this year. The methodology for this assessment can be f...

writtenQuestions.heading: National Wealth Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: What progress she has made with Cabinet colleagues on establishing a National Wealth Fund.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is working at pace to establish the national wealth fund which is critical to our growth and green energy missions. It will crowd in investment from the private sector into the industries of the future and create jobs across the country. Th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Royal Mail: Universal Service Obligation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent assessment he has made of the proposals by prospective buyers of Royal Mail.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government acknowledges the undertakings set out in the offer to shareholders which show that the prospective buyers are alert to the importance of Royal Mail and the special place it has in the UK.We are engaging with the bidder to understand better ...
